source.title: Documented seed: narrative map agenda page
source.source_note: This extraction is reconstructed from the documented OpenClaw slide pattern summaries, not from the original slide screenshot.

claim: Establish narrative hierarchy with one dominant chapter and two supporting chapters rather than a flat agenda list.
visual_anchor: A dominant chapter card paired with a supporting chapter stack creates the agenda's focal hierarchy.

composition.structure: One dominant left chapter card, one right supporting stack, and one full-width bottom decision cue band.
composition.alignment_logic: Left dominant card and right stack share the same top and bottom frame; the bottom cue aligns to the full content width.
composition.asymmetry: Narrative priority is shown by scale difference, not by breaking the shared column alignment.
composition.image_usage: No hero image is needed; card hierarchy and spacing carry the page.
highlight_grammar: subtle border and shadow treatment on cards
decision cue band highlighted without overpowering the page

components.position_note: Two aligned cards on the right

why_it_works: The page stages the story instead of listing chapters with equal weight.
Shared top and bottom boundaries keep the asymmetry disciplined.
The bottom cue turns the agenda into a decision-oriented opening page.
anti_patterns: Three equal cards that flatten chapter priority.
A bottom cue floating independently from the main frame.
Misaligned left and right columns that make the page feel sloppy.

adaptation_notes: Keep the dominant-versus-supporting hierarchy even if the chapter count changes.
Use this pattern only when the deck benefits from staged narrative framing.
